overview: The Gemara analyzes the Mishnah's prohibition on a lender living in the borrower's courtyard for free or hiring slaves at a discount, discussing whether this applies to all courtyards and slaves, and the status of forced labor.

sugyaBoundaries.gist: The Gemara analyzes the Mishnah's prohibition on a lender living in the borrower's courtyard for free or hiring slaves at a discount, discussing whether this applies to all courtyards and slaves, and the status of forced labor.

steps.title: רַב יוֹסֵף בַּר חָמָא's response: רַב's teaching
steps.stepSummary: Rav Yosef bar Ḥama responds that he follows Rav's teaching that seizing a slave is permitted because the owner is satisfied that the slave's habits are not undone.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב יוֹסֵף בַּר חָמָא cites רַב to justify seizing a slave. This is a setup line introducing a new source.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַב permits seizing a slave because the owner benefits from the slave maintaining work habits. This logic applies to all slaves, not just דָּרִי.

keyTerms.meaning: it is satisfactory for him that his slave's habits not be undone

steps.whatToRemember: The owner benefits from the slave's continued work habits.
steps.whyThisMatters: It provides a broader justification for seizing slaves.

phrases.english: It is pleasant for him that his slave's work not be undone.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֲמַר לֵיהּ: אֲנָא, כִּי הָא דְּרַב דָנִיאֵל בַּר רַב קַטִּינָא

rashi.hebrew: דלא נסתרי עבדיה - שלא ירגיל להיות בטל ויתעצל נסתרי התי"ו משמשת כדרך המתפעלים בתיבת שי"ן וסמ"ך כאשר יתפעל שר ויאמר תשתרר שהתי"ו מתמצעת להפסיק אותיות השורש וכאשר יאמר בגיזרת סף הסתופף מגיזרת שח השתוחח וכן בלשון גמרא משתרי מיסתמיך אשתהי אישתקיל:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the phrase דלא נסתרי עבדיה means the owner benefits because the slave does not become accustomed to idleness and laziness, and he clarifies the grammatical structure of the Aramaic verb נסתרי as a passive form.

tosafot.hebrew: ניחא ליה דלא ניסתרי עבדיה - פי' בערוך. לשון סתירה וקלקול וא"ת בלאו האי טעמא נמי פטור דהא תנן (ב"ק ד' צו:) גזל עבדים והזקינו משלם כשעת הגזילה אבל שכר פעולתם לא משלם ויש לומר דיש לחלק בין נחת אדעתא דגזלנותא לנחת אדעתא דמלאכה כדמפליג התם גבי התוקף ספינתו של חבירו ועשה בה מלאכה:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות cites the ערוך to define נסתרי as meaning spoilage or deterioration, and addresses a potential objection that a thief is already exempt from paying for the slave's labor based on a ברייתא in בבא קמא, by distinguishing between labor performed for the thief's own benefit versus labor performed for the owner's benefit, similar to the case of forcing someone's ship to do work.

meforshim.interplaySummary: While רש״י focuses on the practical benefit of preventing the slave's laziness, תוספות delves into the legal distinction between labor benefiting the thief versus the owner to resolve a conflict with a ruling in בבא קמא.

steps.title: רָבָא's final challenge: appearance of interest
steps.stepSummary: Rava argues that Rav's teaching applies only when no money is owed, but since Rav Yosef bar Ḥama is owed money, it looks like interest.
steps.whatsHappening: רָבָא presses his father with a final objection based on the appearance of interest. This is a challenge line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רָבָא distinguishes between seizing a slave when no debt is owed (permitted by רַב) and when a debt is owed (forbidden as interest). He cites רַב נַחְמָן's teaching about paying rent to support this. רַב יוֹסֵף בַּר חָמָא retracts.

keyTerms.meaning: it has the appearance of interest

steps.whatToRemember: The key distinction is whether a debt is involved, which creates the appearance of interest.
steps.whyThisMatters: It resolves the case by applying the interest prohibition to forced labor when a debt is involved.

phrases.english: He said to him:

phrases.aramaic: הָנֵי מִילֵּי הֵיכָא דְּלָא מַסֵּיק בֵּיהּ זוּזֵי.
phrases.english: These matters, where he does not collect a dinar from him.

phrases.aramaic: מָר כֵּיוָן דְּמַסֵּיק בְּהוּ זוּזֵי מִחֲזֵי כְּרִבִּית.
phrases.english: The Master, since he collects dinars from him, appears as interest.

phrases.aramaic: דְּאָמַר רַב יוֹסֵף בַּר מִנְיוֹמֵי אָמַר רַב נַחְמָן:
phrases.english: As Rav Yosef bar Minyumi says, Rav Naḥman says:

phrases.aramaic: אַף עַל פִּי שֶׁאָמְרוּ הַדָּר בַּחֲצַר
phrases.english: Even though they said, one who dwells in the courtyard

phrases.aramaic: חֲבֵירוֹ שֶׁלֹּא מִדַּעְתּוֹ אֵינוֹ צָרִיךְ לְהַעֲלוֹת לוֹ שָׂכָר.
phrases.english: of his friend, without his knowledge, does not need to bring him rent.

phrases.aramaic: הִלְוָהוּ וְדָר בַּחֲצֵירוֹ צָרִיךְ לְהַעֲלוֹת לוֹ שָׂכָר.
phrases.english: He lent him and dwells in his courtyard, needs to bring him rent.
He said to him: I retract from myself.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֲמַר לֵיהּ: הָנֵי מִילֵּי הֵיכָא דְּלָא מַסֵּיק בֵּיהּ זוּזֵי

steps.title: אַבָּיֵי and רָבָא on removing interest in wheat
steps.stepSummary: Abaye and Rava disagree on how much wheat to remove when interest is paid in excess wheat.
steps.whatsHappening: A new case is introduced about removing interest paid in kind. This is a setup line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: אַבָּיֵי holds that only the market value of the interest is removed, while the excess is considered a discount. רָבָא holds that all the wheat is removed because it all came as interest.
steps.whatToRemember: The dispute is about whether excess payment is considered a discount or part of the interest.
steps.whyThisMatters: It establishes different approaches to calculating and removing interest.

phrases.aramaic: אָמַר אַבָּיֵי: הַאי מַאן דְּמַסֵּיק זוּזֵי דְרִיבִּיתָא בְּחַבְרֵיהּ
phrases.english: Abaye said: This one who collects a dinar of interest from his fellow

phrases.aramaic: וְקָא אָזְלִי חִטֵּי אַרְבְּעָה גְּרִיוֵי בְּזוּזָא
phrases.english: and wheat goes four gerivei for a dinar
in the market, and he gave him five,
when we remove from him –
four we remove from him.

phrases.aramaic: אִידַּךְ – אוֹזוֹלֵי הוּא דְּקָא מוֹזֵיל גַּבֵּיהּ.
phrases.english: The other – it is a discount that he is granting him.
Rava said: Five we remove from him,
because from the beginning it came to him in the law of interest.

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר אַבָּיֵי: הַאי מַאן דְּמַסֵּיק זוּזֵי דְרִיבִּיתָא

rashi.hebrew: כי מפקינן מיניה - הרבית שלקח כדרבי אלעזר דאמר רבית קצוצה יוצאה בדיינים דקיימא לן כוותיה לקמן (עמוד ב'):
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the removal of interest here follows רבי אלעזר's ruling that ריבית קצוצה is extracted by the courts, which is the accepted הלכה as established later in the sugya.

steps.title: אַבָּיֵי and רָבָא on removing interest in a cloak
steps.stepSummary: Abaye and Rava disagree on whether to remove a cloak given as interest payment.
steps.whatsHappening: Another case is introduced about removing interest paid in a physical object. This is a setup line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: אַבָּיֵי holds that the cloak is not removed because it is considered a sale. רָבָא holds that it is removed to prevent the appearance that the cloak is interest.
steps.whatToRemember: The dispute is about whether a physical object given as interest is considered a sale or interest.
steps.whyThisMatters: It extends the discussion to non-monetary interest payments.

phrases.aramaic: וְאָמַר אַבָּיֵי: הַאי מַאן דְּמַסֵּיק אַרְבְּעָה זוּזֵי דְרִיבִּיתָא
phrases.english: And Abaye said: This one who collects four dinars of interest
from his fellow and he gave him a cloak within them,
when we extract from him –
four we extract from him,
a cloak we do not extract from him.
Rava said: A cloak we extract from him.

phrases.aramaic: מַאי טַעְמָא, כִּי הֵיכִי דְּלָא לֵימְרוּ:
phrases.english: What is the reason, so that they should not say:
A cloak that covers and stands –
a cloak of interest it is.

steps.triggerLanguage: וְאָמַר אַבָּיֵי: הַאי מַאן דְּמַסֵּיק אַרְבְּעָה זוּזֵי

rashi.hebrew: גלימא לא מפקינן מיניה - מקחו קיים וקציצת הרבית יחזיר לבעליו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that according to אבאיי, the cloak remains with the borrower because the transaction is treated as a valid sale, and only the monetary interest is returned to the lender.

steps.title: רָבָא on removing interest via inflated rent
steps.stepSummary: Rava rules that if interest is paid via inflated rent, the full inflated amount is removed.
steps.whatsHappening: A final case is introduced about interest paid through inflated rent. This is a setup line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רָבָא holds that the entire inflated rent is considered interest and must be removed. This is because the lender agreed to the inflated price, effectively accepting the interest.
steps.whatToRemember: The key is that the lender accepted the inflated price, making it interest.
steps.whyThisMatters: It applies the interest prohibition to inflated rental agreements.

phrases.aramaic: אָמַר רָבָא: הַאי מַאן דְּמַסֵּיק תְּרֵיסַר זוּזֵי דְּרִיבִּיתָא
phrases.english: Rava said: This one who collects twelve dinars of interest

phrases.aramaic: בְּחַבְרֵיהּ וְאֹגַר לֵיהּ חָצֵר דְּמִתַּגְרָא בְּעַשְׂרָה
phrases.english: from his fellow and rents him a courtyard that is rented for ten
and he rents it to him for twelve,
when we extract from him –
twelve we extract from him.

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר רָבָא: הַאי מַאן דְּמַסֵּיק תְּרֵיסַר זוּזֵי

steps.title: רַב אַחָא's challenge: changing the rental price
steps.stepSummary: Rav Aḥa challenges Rava, suggesting the lender could argue that the inflated rent was only due to the benefit of not paying, and now he should pay the market rate.
steps.whatsHappening: A final challenge is raised against רָבָא's ruling. This is a challenge line.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַב אַחָא suggests that the lender could claim the inflated rent was only because he was benefiting from not paying rent. Now that he is paying, he should only pay the market rate. The גמרא rejects this, stating that the lender accepted the inflated price.
steps.whatToRemember: The challenge is about whether the lender can change his mind about the rental price.
steps.whyThisMatters: It addresses the issue of retroactive changes to interest payments.

phrases.aramaic: אֲמַר לֵיהּ רַב אַחָא מִדִּפְתִּי לְרָבִינָא:
phrases.english: Rav Aḥa of Difti said to Ravina:
And let him say to him, if I rent it now –
that it was being rooted for me,
phrases.notes: Rooted here means the borrower was benefiting from the loan.

phrases.english: now that it is not being rooted for me –
as everyone in the world rents, so I rent,
because he said to him: You considered and accepted.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֲמַר לֵיהּ רַב אַחָא מִדִּפְתִּי לְרָבִינָא

rashi.hebrew: ולימא ליה כי אגרא אנא - כששכרתיה אני בדמים יקרים הללו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the lender's claim is that he originally rented out the item at these high prices.

rashi.hebrew: דהוה קא מישתרשי לי - בריוח היתה באה אלי:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the phrase 'מישרשי לי' means the lender was receiving a profit or benefit from the arrangement.
